interaction_log.prompt: How can I add a flag to an EM function to optionally skip the eigenvalue constraint or fix mixing proportions, so I can run ablations independently?
interaction_log.response_summary: Used for implementation assistance: suggested use_eigenvalue_constraint and learn_pi parameters in run_em. I implemented the full run_em, e_step, m_step with these flags myself; the choice of which two components to ablate (eigenvalue constraint and learned pi) was entirely my own.
interaction_log.student_modification: I chose to ablate (1) eigenvalue constraint and (2) learned mixing proportions. I wrote the ablation code and the plot/table logic myself. The reasoning for why these two components is my own.

interaction_log.prompt: Help me phrase an interpretation paragraph for an ablation where removing the constraint slightly improved NMI on our toy data.
interaction_log.response_summary: Used for writing assistance: suggested framing (constraint as regulariser, effect subtle on easy data). I wrote the final interpretation paragraphs myself to match our actual results (0.9094→0.9156, 0.9094→0.9404 etc.) and to state that ablation choices and explanations are my own.
interaction_log.student_modification: All interpretation text was written by me; I only used the LLM for phrasing ideas. The decision to ablate eigenvalue constraint and learned π, and the explanation of what each reveals, are my own.
